channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: Nov 2025: My Personal AI Stack—Pros, Cons, and Pitfalls
executive_summary: In November 2025, Nate B Jones reveals his personal AI stack, highlighting the nuanced reality behind AI tool usage. He emphasizes the importance of selecting the right AI models for specific tasks, such as using Claude for writing and GPT-5 for analysis, while also incorporating tools like Perplexity and Grok into his daily operations.
bullets: Nate B Jones states, 'the common story is that everyone uses ChatGPT' but the reality is more complex. This highlights that while ChatGPT is popular, it’s not the only tool available, and understanding the strengths of various models is crucial for effective AI use. Think of it like choosing the right tool for a job; you wouldn’t use a hammer to screw in a lightbulb.

Nate separates thinking from writing by using different large language models. This means he uses one model to brainstorm ideas and another to draft text, which allows for clearer and more organized outputs. It’s like cooking a meal where you prepare the ingredients separately before combining them for the final dish.

Claude is used by Nate for writing, Excel, and PowerPoint tasks that feel human. This suggests that Claude excels in generating text that mimics human-like communication, making it ideal for presentations and reports. Imagine using a personal assistant who can draft your emails and presentations just like you would, but faster.

Nate identifies GPT-5 as his go-to model for analysis and structured reasoning. This model is particularly effective for tasks that require logical thinking and data interpretation, making it a powerful ally for decision-making. Think of it like having a skilled analyst who can sift through data and present clear insights, rather than just raw numbers.

Nate incorporates tools like Perplexity, Grok, and Comet into his daily AI operations. Each of these tools serves a unique purpose, enhancing his overall workflow and efficiency. It’s like having a toolbox where each tool is designed for a specific task, ensuring that you can tackle any job that comes your way.

Nate emphasizes that mastering AI tools is about knowing when and why to trust each model. This understanding allows users to leverage the strengths of different models effectively, rather than relying on a one-size-fits-all approach. Think of it like a chess game where knowing the strengths of each piece can lead to a winning strategy.

The video suggests that many users currently have a backward approach to evaluating code hygiene, with 80% of tests focusing on functionality and only 20% on hygiene. This imbalance can lead to poor code quality over time, making it essential to prioritize code structure and naming conventions. Imagine a house built with a beautiful facade but with a crumbling foundation underneath.

Nate mentions that implementation costs for AI tools are going to zero, which means that accessing advanced AI capabilities will become increasingly affordable. This democratization of technology allows more individuals and businesses to leverage AI without significant financial barriers. It’s like how smartphones became accessible to everyone, changing the way we communicate and work.

Nate points out that tools like Grok can help streamline daily tasks, making them more efficient. This efficiency can save time and reduce the cognitive load on users, allowing them to focus on more strategic activities. Think of it like having a smart calendar that not only reminds you of appointments but also suggests the best times for meetings based on your schedule.

Nate's personal AI stack is tailored to his specific needs, showcasing the importance of customization in AI workflows. By selecting tools that align with his tasks, he maximizes productivity and effectiveness. It’s like customizing a suit to fit perfectly rather than wearing something off the rack that doesn’t quite fit.

Nate's approach to using multiple models reflects a strategic mindset that prioritizes quality over quantity in AI tool usage. This strategy helps him achieve better outcomes in his work, ensuring that he uses the right model for the right task. Think of it like a chef who knows which knife to use for each ingredient, resulting in a more refined dish.

Nate's workflow illustrates that the future of AI is not just about using one tool but about integrating multiple tools for a comprehensive approach. This integration allows for greater flexibility and adaptability in tackling various challenges. It’s like a well-orchestrated symphony where each instrument plays its part to create a harmonious sound.
key_takeaways: Set a rule to use different AI models for thinking and writing tasks. This separation will enhance clarity and organization in your outputs. If you skip this, you may end up with jumbled ideas and poorly structured content.

Prioritize code hygiene in your evaluation tests, aiming for at least 50% of tests to check for naming, structure, and function length. This focus will improve code quality and maintainability. Ignoring this could lead to technical debt and increased bugs over time.

Explore the integration of tools like Perplexity, Grok, and Comet into your daily operations. This will streamline your workflow and enhance efficiency. Without these tools, you may find yourself bogged down by repetitive tasks.

By the end of the year, aim to have a customized AI stack that fits your specific needs. This tailored approach will maximize your productivity and effectiveness in your work. Failing to customize may leave you using tools that don’t serve your best interests.

technical_terms.Claude: A large language model designed for generating human-like text, particularly useful for writing tasks. Think of it like a personal assistant who can draft your emails and presentations just like you would, but faster.
technical_terms.GPT-5: The fifth version of the Generative Pre-trained Transformer model, known for its advanced analytical and reasoning capabilities. Think of it like having a skilled analyst who can sift through data and present clear insights, rather than just raw numbers.
technical_terms.Perplexity: An AI tool that helps streamline information retrieval and processing tasks. Think of it like a smart librarian who can quickly find the exact book or information you need.
technical_terms.Grok: An AI tool designed to enhance productivity by automating repetitive tasks. Think of it like a smart calendar that not only reminds you of appointments but also suggests the best times for meetings based on your schedule.
technical_terms.Comet: A tool that aids in tracking and managing machine learning experiments. Think of it like a lab notebook that keeps track of all your experiments and results in one organized place.
technical_terms.Code Hygiene: The practice of maintaining clean, well-structured, and readable code. Think of it like keeping your workspace tidy; a clean environment helps you work more efficiently and reduces errors.
technical_terms.AI Stack: A collection of AI tools and models used together to accomplish various tasks. Think of it like a toolbox where each tool is designed for a specific job, ensuring you can tackle any task that comes your way.
technical_terms.Implementation Costs: The expenses associated with deploying AI tools and technologies. Think of it like the initial investment in a new kitchen; the upfront costs can lead to greater efficiency and savings in the long run.
technical_terms.Large Language Model: A type of AI model trained on vast amounts of text data to understand and generate human-like language. Think of it like a sponge soaking up knowledge from books, articles, and conversations.
technical_terms.Structured Reasoning: The ability to analyze information logically and draw conclusions based on that analysis. Think of it like solving a puzzle where each piece must fit together to reveal the complete picture.
